gaps: No test covers the Sensors & Presence settings category for Poly Voyager Free 20 at all.
No test covers Poly Lens Cloud policy enforcement (locked policy values) being reflected in the Poly Studio Mobile app.
No test covers the scenario where a cloud-configured locked policy value for device settings is compared against the value displayed in the Poly Studio Mobile app UI.
No test covers Device Settings page behavior under managed/policy-enforced configurations for Voyager Free 20.
No test covers the specific navigation path: Home → [Voyager Free 20] → Device Settings → Sensors & Presence for verifying cloud policy values.

generated_testcases.title: TC-SENSORS-POLICY-001: Verify Locked Policy Value for Wear Detection Sensor on Voyager Free 20
steps: Precondition: Voyager Free 20 is enrolled in Poly Lens Cloud with a locked policy that sets 'Wear Detection Sensor' = ON.
Precondition: Device is connected to the Poly Studio Mobile app.
Open Poly Studio Mobile app on the paired smartphone.
Tap the Voyager Free 20 device name from the Home screen.
Navigate to: Device Settings → Sensors & Presence.
Locate the 'Wear Detection Sensor' toggle.
Observe the toggle state and check if it is grayed out / non-interactive (locked by policy).
Note the displayed value (ON or OFF).
generated_testcases.expected: The 'Wear Detection Sensor' toggle displays ON (matching the Poly Lens Cloud locked policy value). The toggle is grayed out and cannot be changed by the user. A lock icon or policy indicator is visible next to the setting.

generated_testcases.title: TC-SENSORS-POLICY-002: Verify Locked Policy Value for In-Ear Detection on Voyager Free 20
steps: Precondition: Voyager Free 20 is enrolled in Poly Lens Cloud with a locked policy that sets 'In-Ear Detection' = OFF.
Precondition: Device is connected to the Poly Studio Mobile app.
Open Poly Studio Mobile app.
Tap the Voyager Free 20 device name from the Home screen.
Navigate to: Device Settings → Sensors & Presence.
Locate the 'In-Ear Detection' toggle.
Attempt to toggle the switch ON.
Observe the toggle behavior and displayed value.
generated_testcases.expected: The 'In-Ear Detection' toggle displays OFF (matching the Poly Lens Cloud locked policy value). The toggle is grayed out and non-interactive. Attempting to toggle it has no effect. A lock icon or policy indicator is visible.

generated_testcases.title: TC-SENSORS-POLICY-003: Verify Locked Policy Value for Auto Pause/Play on Voyager Free 20
steps: Precondition: Voyager Free 20 is enrolled in Poly Lens Cloud with a locked policy that sets 'Auto Pause/Play' = ON.
Precondition: Device is connected to the Poly Studio Mobile app.
Open Poly Studio Mobile app.
Tap the Voyager Free 20 device name from the Home screen.
Navigate to: Device Settings → Sensors & Presence.
Locate the 'Auto Pause/Play' toggle.
Observe the toggle state.
Close the app completely (force kill).
Reopen Poly Studio Mobile app and navigate back to: Device Settings → Sensors & Presence.
Observe the 'Auto Pause/Play' toggle state again.
generated_testcases.expected: The 'Auto Pause/Play' toggle displays ON both before and after app restart. The toggle is grayed out and non-interactive. The locked policy value persists across app lifecycle events.

generated_testcases.title: TC-SENSORS-POLICY-004: Verify Locked Policy Value for Headset Sensors Master Toggle on Voyager Free 20
steps: Precondition: Voyager Free 20 is enrolled in Poly Lens Cloud with a locked policy that sets 'Headset Sensors' master toggle = OFF.
Precondition: Device is connected to the Poly Studio Mobile app.
Open Poly Studio Mobile app.
Tap the Voyager Free 20 device name from the Home screen.
Navigate to: Device Settings → Sensors & Presence.
Locate the 'Headset Sensors' master toggle (if applicable) or the first sensor toggle in the list.
Observe the displayed state.
Disconnect Bluetooth from the device (turn off Bluetooth on phone or put device out of range).
Reconnect Bluetooth and wait for the app to re-establish connection.
Navigate back to: Device Settings → Sensors & Presence.
Observe the toggle state again.
generated_testcases.expected: The 'Headset Sensors' master toggle displays OFF (matching the Poly Lens Cloud locked policy value) both before and after Bluetooth disconnect/reconnect. The toggle is grayed out and non-interactive. All sub-settings under Sensors & Presence are also locked and reflect their respective policy values.

generated_testcases.title: TC-SENSORS-POLICY-005: Verify Locked Policy Value Persists After Device Power Cycle for Voyager Free 20
steps: Precondition: Voyager Free 20 is enrolled in Poly Lens Cloud with a locked policy that sets 'Wear Detection Sensor' = ON and 'Auto Pause/Play' = OFF.
Precondition: Device is connected to the Poly Studio Mobile app.
Open Poly Studio Mobile app.
Tap the Voyager Free 20 device name from the Home screen.
Navigate to: Device Settings → Sensors & Presence.
Verify 'Wear Detection Sensor' shows ON and 'Auto Pause/Play' shows OFF.
Place the earbuds in the charge case and close the lid.
Wait 10 seconds.
Remove earbuds from the charge case and allow them to reconnect to the phone.
Open Poly Studio Mobile app.
Navigate to: Device Settings → Sensors & Presence.
Observe the values for 'Wear Detection Sensor' and 'Auto Pause/Play'.
generated_testcases.expected: After device power cycle (case close/open), 'Wear Detection Sensor' still displays ON and 'Auto Pause/Play' still displays OFF, matching the Poly Lens Cloud locked policy values. Both toggles remain grayed out and non-interactive.

generated_testcases.title: TC-SENSORS-POLICY-006: Verify Locked Policy Value Override — User Cannot Change Sensor Setting in App
steps: Precondition: Voyager Free 20 is enrolled in Poly Lens Cloud with a locked policy that sets 'Wear Detection Sensor' = OFF.
Precondition: Device is connected to the Poly Studio Mobile app.
Open Poly Studio Mobile app.
Tap the Voyager Free 20 device name from the Home screen.
Navigate to: Device Settings → Sensors & Presence.
Confirm 'Wear Detection Sensor' shows OFF and is grayed out.
Attempt to tap the 'Wear Detection Sensor' toggle 5 times rapidly.
Attempt to long-press the 'Wear Detection Sensor' toggle for 3 seconds.
Attempt to swipe the toggle.
Observe the toggle state after each attempt.
generated_testcases.expected: The 'Wear Detection Sensor' toggle remains OFF and grayed out after all user interaction attempts (tap, rapid tap, long-press, swipe). The locked policy value cannot be overridden through any UI interaction. No toast or error message indicating a change is displayed.

generated_testcases.title: TC-SENSORS-POLICY-007: Verify Locked Policy Value for All Sensors & Presence Settings Simultaneously on Voyager Free 20
steps: Precondition: Voyager Free 20 is enrolled in Poly Lens Cloud with locked policies: 'Headset Sensors' = ON, 'Wear Detection Sensor' = ON, 'In-Ear Detection' = OFF, 'Auto Pause/Play' = ON.
Precondition: Device is connected to the Poly Studio Mobile app.
Open Poly Studio Mobile app.
Tap the Voyager Free 20 device name from the Home screen.
Navigate to: Device Settings → Sensors & Presence.
Observe all available sensor toggles and their states.
Take a screenshot of the Sensors & Presence screen.
Compare each toggle's displayed value against the expected locked policy values from Poly Lens Cloud.
generated_testcases.expected: All Sensors & Presence settings match their respective Poly Lens Cloud locked policy values: 'Headset Sensors' = ON, 'Wear Detection Sensor' = ON, 'In-Ear Detection' = OFF, 'Auto Pause/Play' = ON. All toggles are grayed out and non-interactive. Lock/policy indicators are visible on each locked setting.

generated_testcases.title: TC-SENSORS-POLICY-008: Verify Locked Policy Value After App Update for Voyager Free 20
steps: Precondition: Voyager Free 20 is enrolled in Poly Lens Cloud with a locked policy that sets 'Wear Detection Sensor' = ON.
Precondition: Device is connected to the Poly Studio Mobile app (current version).
Open Poly Studio Mobile app.
Tap the Voyager Free 20 device name from the Home screen.
Navigate to: Device Settings → Sensors & Presence.
Verify 'Wear Detection Sensor' shows ON and is grayed out.
Close the app.
Update the Poly Studio Mobile app to the latest version from the app store.
Open the updated Poly Studio Mobile app.
Tap the Voyager Free 20 device name from the Home screen.
Navigate to: Device Settings → Sensors & Presence.
Observe the 'Wear Detection Sensor' toggle state.
generated_testcases.expected: After app update, the 'Wear Detection Sensor' toggle still displays ON (matching the Poly Lens Cloud locked policy value) and remains grayed out. The policy enforcement survives an app update.

generated_testcases.title: TC-SENSORS-POLICY-009: Verify Locked Policy Value When No Policy Is Configured (Baseline Negative Test)
steps: Precondition: Voyager Free 20 is NOT enrolled in any Poly Lens Cloud policy (no locked values).
Precondition: Device is connected to the Poly Studio Mobile app.
Open Poly Studio Mobile app.
Tap the Voyager Free 20 device name from the Home screen.
Navigate to: Device Settings → Sensors & Presence.
Observe all sensor toggles (Wear Detection Sensor, In-Ear Detection, Auto Pause/Play, etc.).
Verify that toggles are interactive (not grayed out).
Toggle 'Wear Detection Sensor' from ON to OFF.
Navigate away to Device Settings main page.
Navigate back to: Device Settings → Sensors & Presence.
Observe the 'Wear Detection Sensor' state.
generated_testcases.expected: When no Poly Lens Cloud policy is configured, all Sensors & Presence toggles are interactive (not grayed out). The user can freely change settings. The 'Wear Detection Sensor' toggle reflects the user's change (OFF) and persists after navigation. No lock icons or policy indicators are visible.

generated_testcases.title: TC-SENSORS-POLICY-010: Verify Locked Policy Value Sync After Policy Change in Poly Lens Cloud
steps: Precondition: Voyager Free 20 is enrolled in Poly Lens Cloud with a locked policy that sets 'Auto Pause/Play' = ON.
Precondition: Device is connected to the Poly Studio Mobile app.
Open Poly Studio Mobile app.
Tap the Voyager Free 20 device name from the Home screen.
Navigate to: Device Settings → Sensors & Presence.
Verify 'Auto Pause/Play' shows ON and is grayed out.
Leave the app open and visible on the Sensors & Presence screen.
On a separate device, log into Poly Lens Cloud admin console.
Change the locked policy for Voyager Free 20: set 'Auto Pause/Play' = OFF.
Save and push the policy update.
Wait up to 2 minutes for the policy to sync to the device.
Observe the 'Auto Pause/Play' toggle in the Poly Studio Mobile app.
If no automatic update occurs, close and reopen the app, then navigate back to: Device Settings → Sensors & Presence.
generated_testcases.expected: The 'Auto Pause/Play' toggle updates to OFF (matching the updated Poly Lens Cloud locked policy) either automatically or after app restart. The toggle remains grayed out and non-interactive. The app reflects the latest cloud policy within a reasonable sync timeframe.
